description: Natural technical-consultant tasks. Case titles, tags, and answer keys are never sent to the evaluated agent.

cases.title: Conditional response redaction in PingAuthorize
cases.prompt: We run PingAuthorize 11.1 as an API security gateway. A GET /customers/{id} response contains ssn and dateOfBirth. Members of the fraud-investigators group may receive both fields; everyone else who is allowed to call the API must receive the same response with those two fields removed. Describe the policy structure, the attributes or resolvers it needs, where the response modification belongs, and how you would test the allow-versus-redact behavior before deployment.

cases.title: Cache an external lookup in a PingAM journey
cases.prompt: A PingAM 8.1 authentication journey has two Scripted Decision nodes that need the same risk value from an external HTTP service. We are currently making the call twice and occasionally get inconsistent results. Show the supported way to cache the value for reuse later in the journey, explain the lifetime and scope of that cached value, and call out what must not be stored there. Include a compact Groovy-oriented example or pseudocode.

cases.title: Build and deploy a PingFederate IdP adapter
cases.prompt: For PingFederate 13.1 we need an IdP adapter that validates a proprietary HTTP header, returns employeeId and assuranceLevel in its contract, and exposes two configurable fields to administrators. Outline the SDK interfaces and lifecycle methods to implement, the descriptor/configuration work, how the extension is packaged and deployed, and the server restart or reload implications. Please distinguish implementation work from the later admin-console instance configuration.

cases.title: Diagnose PingDirectory replication delay
cases.prompt: In a three-server PingDirectory 11.1 topology, one replica is several hours behind but still accepts reads. Give me a safe diagnostic sequence that identifies whether the problem is connectivity, replication backlog, or a server-local issue. Include the commands or monitor data you would inspect, the evidence needed before reinitializing anything, and the least disruptive recovery choices.

cases.title: Configure PingFederate as PingAccess token provider
cases.prompt: We are moving a PingAccess 9.1 deployment from a locally configured signing key to PingFederate as the token provider. Describe the configuration on both sides, the metadata/endpoints and credentials PingAccess needs, how certificate trust and key rollover should be handled, and a verification plan that does not interrupt existing protected applications.

cases.title: Switch device during a PingID authentication
cases.prompt: Our custom sign-on page uses PingID API 4.9 or later. A user starts a push to their default phone, then chooses another paired device before approving. What exact operation sequence should the application follow, which values must be carried between calls, and why is one cancellation step mandatory for a mobile-app push? Cover both device-selection mode and default-device mode.

cases.title: Bootstrap a least-privilege PingOne Worker app
cases.prompt: I need a non-interactive service to list users in one PingOne environment and nothing else. Starting with console access, give me the least-privilege Worker app setup, the token request including the correct regional hostname pattern, and the API request shape. Also explain how I should rotate the Worker secret without baking it into source code.

cases.title: Choose safe DaVinci variable scopes
cases.prompt: A DaVinci sign-on flow calls a fraud service and then branches twice on the returned risk score. The team currently writes the API secret, raw response, score, and user-facing explanation into global variables so subflows can read them. Redesign the variable usage so only the intended values cross flow and subflow boundaries, secrets are not exposed, and failure paths remain testable. Be precise about which variable scopes you would use and why.

cases.title: Secure an AIC scripted custom endpoint
cases.prompt: In PingOne Advanced Identity Cloud we need a small server-side REST endpoint that returns a caller-specific summary from managed objects. Explain how to create and invoke a scripted custom endpoint, what request/context data the script receives, how to enforce authorization rather than trusting user-supplied identifiers, and how to return useful HTTP errors without leaking object data. Include the endpoint URL pattern and a compact script skeleton.

cases.title: Inject and cache service tokens in PingGateway
cases.prompt: A PingGateway 2026 route fronts a downstream API that requires a service access token from PingAM. Provide a route-level design that obtains tokens with client_credentials, injects the bearer token into outbound requests, avoids requesting a new token on every call, and keeps the client secret out of the route JSON. Name the relevant PingGateway objects and show how they fit together.

cases.title: Verify and rebuild a degraded PingDS index
cases.prompt: PingDS 8.1 reports that the mail equality index is degraded on one replica. Searches still work but are slow. Give a cautious runbook to confirm the index is actually inconsistent, choose online versus offline rebuild, monitor the work, and verify correctness afterward. Include the relevant command names and how operating on one replica affects service availability.

cases.title: Choose and rehearse ForgeOps DS restore
cases.prompt: For a ForgeOps 2026.2 production deployment, compare a directory restore based on dsbackup with a Kubernetes volume-snapshot restore. I need the prerequisites, what each option restores, coordination or outage implications, and a rehearsal plan that proves the recovery point without risking the live namespace. End with criteria for choosing one method over the other.

cases.title: Promote PingAuthorize policy safely
cases.prompt: We need to promote a tested PingAuthorize 11.1 policy change from integration to production, but the environments use different external attribute-service URLs and credentials. Describe how to keep the policy and Trust Framework portable, where environment-specific values should live, how to package and deploy the change, and how to detect unintended policy drift before and after promotion.

cases.title: Build an importable PingAuthorize workload-governance policy pack for Kong
cases.prompt:
We are starting with a newly installed PingAuthorize Server and Policy Editor. First determine the current supported product and Policy Editor version from official Ping documentation, state the version and evidence, and use that same version consistently for all configuration, policy formats, APIs, and instructions. Produce a complete implementation pack for a Kong Gateway integration: (1) an idempotent shell script that configures PingAuthorize and the Policy Editor after installation, including the supported Kong sideband integration, token validation, Trust Framework inputs, secrets, and any policy-request mappings that are actually applicable; (2) the policy configuration as JSON and a supported path that turns it into a genuinely importable Policy Editor artifact—if a hand-authored generic JSON document is not itself an importable snapshot, say so and have the script use supported Policy Editor APIs and export a .snapshot rather than mislabelling it; (3) current-version Policy Editor GUI and API instructions for importing the policies, plus the distinct production deployment-package step; and (4) customer-facing instructions for Kong that specify the transport/plugin configuration and the exact headers, token claims, certificate data, data types, allowed values, and trust/spoofing controls needed for evaluation. Keep secrets out of source and use explicit placeholders for environment-specific values.

The policy set must be deny-by-default and implement these controls with clear precedence: UC-01 certificate-bound workload identity—Keyfactor-issued mTLS certificate plus OAuth client identity; permit only when the certificate is present, trusted, in-date, not revoked, and cryptographically bound to the token, and deny missing, invalid, revoked, or mismatched credentials. UC-02 business-unit isolation—NEM, WEM, and Gas services can access only resources in the same business unit unless an explicit cross-BU allow rule applies. UC-03 risk-aware service restriction—SIEM serviceRiskScore, hostCompromised, and anomalyScore remove write access or deny access at documented thresholds. UC-04 high-impact dual control—deployment, configuration, administrative, and OT-control operations require an approved privileged workload, an approved change window, and acceptable risk. UC-05 cross-zone governance—sourceZone, destinationZone, and resourceZone must match an explicit zone-to-zone allow matrix. UC-06 break-glass lockdown—when incidentState is active, only named emergency and incident-response services retain access; this global gate has the highest precedence without bypassing certificate/token validity.

Include the policy hierarchy and combining algorithms, Trust Framework attribute/resolver design, example environment data for identities, BU mappings, cross-BU exceptions, risk thresholds, change windows, zone matrix, and emergency identities, plus a positive/negative test matrix covering all six use cases. Distinguish values that PingAuthorize obtains natively from the Kong ping-auth sideband request (access token, client certificate, request headers, method/path) from values Kong or a trusted PIP must derive. In particular, do not claim that certificate SAN, revocation status, or token-to-certificate thumbprint matching is native unless the cited current-version interface actually supplies it. Cite the exact Ping documentation pages used as full URLs.

cases.title: Ambiguous product and version
cases.prompt: Since the upgrade, access-token validation fails for about five minutes whenever signing keys roll over. Tell me exactly which cache and timeout settings to change so rollover is seamless.

cases.title: Non-Ping host agent should not trigger Ping docs
cases.prompt: The monitoring agent on one of our Linux build hosts stopped checking in after the OS was upgraded. Give me the commands to repair it.
